sections.heading: Pathway
sections.content: [{"pathway_id": "seminoma_stage1_ia_ib", "title": "Pure Seminoma Stage IA/IB: Primary Treatment After Orchiectomy", "branching_basis": ["Pathologic stage (IA vs IB)", "Patient preference and ability to comply with surveillance", "Risk factors including tumor size and rete testis invasion", "Contraindications to specific treatments"], "nodes": [{"criteria": "Stage IA or IB pT1-pT3 seminoma after radical inguinal orchiectomy", "options": [{"type": "surveillance", "label": "Active Surveillance (strongly preferred)", "preference": "preferred", "recommendation": "80%-85% of patients with stage I pure seminoma are cured by orchiectomy alone. Relapse rates range from 15%-20% with most relapses in infradiaphragmatic lymph nodes. Disease-specific survival approaches 100% irrespective of management strategy [SEM-2, MS-8]."}, {"type": "chemotherapy", "label": "Single-agent Carboplatin (AUC 7 x1 or x2 cycles)", "preference": "other_recommended", "recommendation": "For patients who decline surveillance or for whom surveillance is not reliably good. A randomized trial showed noninferiority of single-dose carboplatin (AUC 7) versus RT with relapse-free survival rates of 94.7% vs 96% at 5 years [MS-9]. Carboplatin was associated with less lethargy and less time off work compared to RT."}, {"type": "radiation_therapy", "label": "Radiation Therapy (20 Gy preferred)", "preference": "other_recommended", "recommendation": "For select patients. Modern RT involves smaller fields and lower doses than historically used. Long-term follow-up studies indicate an increase in late toxicities with radiation treatment including secondary malignancies [SEM-2, MS-10]."}], "adjuvant_logic": {"applies": true, "summary": "For patients not choosing surveillance, adjuvant therapy options include single-agent carboplatin or radiation therapy. Both reduce relapse risk but carry potential late effects. Carboplatin has limited long-term follow-up data. RT associated with increased risk of secondary malignancies and non-cancer-related mortality [MS-10]."}}]}, {"pathway_id": "seminoma_stage_is", "title": "Pure Seminoma Stage IS: Evaluation and Treatment", "branching_basis": ["Persistent elevation of serum tumor markers post-orchiectomy", "Radiographic evidence of metastatic disease", "Marker trajectory"], "nodes": [{"criteria": "Stage IS: persistent elevation of serum tumor markers post-orchiectomy with no radiographic evidence of metastatic disease", "options": [{"type": "evaluation", "label": "Repeat measurements and imaging", "preference": "preferred", "recommendation": "Repeat measurements of serum tumor markers and perform chest/abdomen/pelvis CT with contrast to scan for evaluable disease. Repeat scrotal ultrasound to rule out contralateral testis mass if not conducted in last 3 months [SEM-2]."}, {"type": "systemic_therapy", "label": "First-line Chemotherapy", "preference": "preferred", "recommendation": "Elevated tumor markers increase risk of disease outside retroperitoneum; therefore systemic therapy should be encouraged. Treatment per standard chemotherapy regimens for good-risk disease [SEM-2]."}], "adjuvant_logic": {"applies": false, "summary": "Stage IS represents occult metastatic disease indicated by marker elevation. Patients should not be treated based on elevated LDH alone [SEM-2]."}}]}, {"pathway_id": "seminoma_stage2a_2b", "title": "Pure Seminoma Stage IIA/IIB: Primary Treatment", "branching_basis": ["Lymph node mass size (<3 cm vs 3-5 cm)", "Bulky vs non-bulky disease", "Patient preference and shared decision-making", "Borderline lymph nodes"], "nodes": [{"criteria": "Stage IIA/IIB non-bulky (largest node <3 cm transaxial long axis)", "options": [{"type": "radiation_therapy", "label": "RT (para-aortic and ipsilateral iliac nodes, 30-36 Gy)", "preference": "other_recommended", "recommendation": "For nonbulky disease. RT should include para-aortic and ipsilateral iliac lymph nodes. Dose of 30 Gy for 1-2 cm disease, 36 Gy for 2-3 cm disease [SEM-3]."}, {"type": "systemic_therapy", "label": "BEP x3 or EP x4", "preference": "preferred", "recommendation": "Standard first-line chemotherapy for stage IIA/IIB. Both regimens are preferred [SEM-3]."}, {"type": "surgery", "label": "Nerve-sparing RPLND", "preference": "other_recommended", "recommendation": "Can be considered for stage IIA and non-bulky IIB. Category 2B for 2-3 cm disease. Referral to high-volume center recommended [SEM-3, MS-13]."}, {"type": "combined", "label": "Carboplatin + RT (category 2B)", "preference": "other_recommended", "recommendation": "Based on SAKK 01/10 trial showing 3-year PFS of 93.7%. Limited data beyond 3 years [SEM-3, MS-14]."}], "adjuvant_logic": {"applies": true, "summary": "For RPLND: pN0 surveillance; pN1 surveillance (preferred) or adjuvant chemotherapy; pN2 surveillance or adjuvant chemotherapy; pN3 surveillance (category 2B) or adjuvant chemotherapy (preferred) [SEM-3]."}}, {"criteria": "Stage IIA/IIB bulky (3-5 cm)", "options": [{"type": "systemic_

flowcharts.title: Pure Seminoma Stage IA/IB: Primary Treatment After Orchiectomy

nodes.label: Stage IA or IB pT1-pT3 seminoma after radical inguinal orchiectomy
Single-agent Carboplatin (AUC 7 x1 or x2 cycles)

edges.label: Other recommended (patients declining surveillance, noninferior to RT)

flowcharts.title: Pure Seminoma Stage IS: Evaluation and Treatment

nodes.label: Stage IS: persistent elevation of serum tumor markers post-orchiectomy with no radiographic evidence

edges.label: Perform repeat measurements and imaging to rule out evaluable disease
If markers still elevated, systemic therapy encouraged

flowcharts.title: Pure Seminoma Stage IIA/IIB: Primary Treatment

nodes.label: Stage IIA/IIB non-bulky (largest node <3 cm transaxial long axis)
RT (para-aortic and ipsilateral iliac nodes, 30-36 Gy)
BEP x3 or EP x4 (systemic therapy)

edges.label: Other recommended (category 2B for 2-3 cm, high-volume center)
